title: puccinellia macranthera
intro: Puccinellia macranthera, also known as Big Saltgrass, is a perennial grass-like plant that belongs to the grass family (Poaceae). It is native to the coastal regions of North America, particularly in the western United States and Canada. The plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antiseptic properties. The health benefits of Puccinellia macranthera include treating wounds, skin conditions, and respiratory issues, and it can be prepared into teas, infusions, or tinctures for topical or internal use.
